description: HTML markup language completion data for web development

keywords.description: div - Generic container element for grouping other HTML elements. The most commonly used block-level element for layout and styling.
span - Inline container element for styling parts of text or small sections of content without affecting layout.
p - Paragraph element for text content. Automatically adds margins above and below for proper text spacing.
keywords.example: This is a paragraph of text with proper semantic meaning.

keywords.description: h1 - Main heading element, the largest and most important heading. Use only one per page for best SEO and accessibility.
h2 - Secondary heading element for major sections. Creates a logical document outline hierarchy.
h3 - Third-level heading for subsections. Part of the document outline for accessibility tools.
a - Anchor element for hyperlinks and navigation. Essential for creating interactive web experiences.
img - Image element for displaying pictures and graphics. Always include alt attribute for accessibility.
button - Interactive button element for user actions. More semantic than styled divs for accessibility.
input - Form input element for user data entry. Many different types available for various data formats.
keywords.example: <input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Enter your name" required>

keywords.description: form - Container for form controls like inputs, buttons, and selects. Handles data submission to servers.
ul - Unordered list container for list items where order doesn't matter. Creates bullet points by default.
ol - Ordered list container for numbered or sequenced items where order is important.
li - List item element used inside ul or ol elements. Contains individual list content.
table - Table element for displaying tabular data in rows and columns. Use for actual data, not layout.
h4 - Fourth-level heading for sub-subsections. Maintains document hierarchy and accessibility structure.
h5 - Fifth-level heading for detailed subsections. Less commonly used but important for complex documents.
h6 - Sixth and smallest heading level for the most detailed sections.
header - Semantic element representing introductory content or navigation aids for a section or page.
footer - Semantic element containing information about its section, such as copyright, links, or author info.
nav - Semantic element representing a section of navigation links.
main - Semantic element representing the dominant content of the document body. Only one per page.
section - Semantic element representing a distinct section of content, typically with a heading.
article - Semantic element representing a complete, self-contained composition like blog posts or news articles.
aside - Semantic element for content tangentially related to main content, like sidebars or callout boxes.
figure - Element representing self-contained content, often with a caption, like images, diagrams, or code listings.
figcaption - Caption or legend element for figure content, providing description or title.
details - Interactive element that users can expand/collapse to show/hide additional information.
summary - Clickable heading for details element, serving as the visible title when collapsed.
dialog - Element representing a dialog box or modal window that users can interact with.
mark - Element representing text marked or highlighted for reference purposes, like search results.
strong - Element indicating strong importance, seriousness, or urgency. Typically renders as bold.
em - Element indicating stress emphasis, changing the meaning of the sentence. Typically renders as italic.
small - Element representing side comments like copyright, legal text, or fine print.
sub - Element for subscript text, typically used in mathematical formulas or chemical notation.
sup - Element for superscript text, used for footnotes, mathematical exponents, or ordinal numbers.
code - Element representing a fragment of computer code, styled with monospace font.
pre - Element for preformatted text that preserves whitespace, line breaks, and spacing.
kbd - Element representing keyboard input or user input from other input devices.
var - Element representing a variable in mathematical expressions or programming contexts.
samp - Element representing sample output from a computer program or system.
blockquote - Element for extended quotations from other sources, with optional cite attribute.
q - Element for short inline quotations, automatically adds appropriate quotation marks.
cite - Element representing the title of a cited work like books, papers, songs, or movies.
abbr - Element representing an abbreviation or acronym, with optional title attribute for full form.
dfn - Element representing the defining instance of a term being defined in the content.
keywords.example: HTML is the standard markup language for web pages.

keywords.description: time - Element representing a specific period in time, with optional datetime attribute for machine-readable format.
address - Element providing contact information for its nearest article or body ancestor.
del - Element representing deleted text, typically shown with strikethrough formatting.
ins - Element representing inserted text, typically shown with underline formatting.
br - Line break element forcing text to continue on the next line. Self-closing tag.
hr - Horizontal rule representing a thematic break between content sections. Self-closing tag.
wbr - Word break opportunity element suggesting where text might break across lines. Self-closing tag.
select - Form control for selecting from multiple options, creating dropdown menus.
option - Individual option within select, optgroup, or datalist elements.
optgroup - Element for grouping related options within a select element.
textarea - Multi-line text input control for longer text content like comments or messages.
label - Element providing accessible labels for form controls, improving usability and accessibility.
fieldset - Element for grouping related form controls with an optional legend element.
legend - Caption element for fieldset content, describing the group of form controls.
datalist - Element providing predefined options for input elements with autocomplete functionality.
output - Element representing the result of a calculation or user action within a form.
progress - Element representing the completion progress of a task with visual progress bar.
meter - Element representing a scalar value within a known range or gauge value.
video - Element for embedding video content with built-in playback controls.
audio - Element for embedding audio content with optional playback controls.
keywords.example: <audio controls><source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">Your browser does not support audio.</audio>

keywords.description: source - Element specifying multiple media resources for video, audio, or picture elements. Self-closing.
track - Element providing text tracks for video/audio elements like subtitles or captions. Self-closing.
picture - Element containing multiple source elements for responsive images with different conditions.
canvas - Element providing a drawing surface for 2D graphics and animations via JavaScript.
svg - Element for embedding Scalable Vector Graphics directly in HTML documents.
keywords.example: <svg width="100" height="100"><circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40" fill="red"/></svg>

keywords.description: iframe - Element for embedding another HTML document within the current document.
embed - Element for embedding external content like plugins or multimedia. Self-closing.
object - Element for embedding external resources like images, videos, or other HTML documents.
param - Element defining parameters for object elements. Self-closing.
dl - Description list element containing terms and their descriptions.
dt - Description term element within a description list, defining the term being described.
dd - Description details element providing the description or definition for a term.
thead - Table head element grouping header content in a table.
tbody - Table body element grouping the main content rows in a table.
tfoot - Table footer element grouping footer content like totals or summary information.
tr - Table row element containing table cells (th or td elements).
th - Table header cell element, typically bold and centered, for column or row headers.
td - Table data cell element containing the actual table content.
caption - Element providing a title or explanation for a table, positioned above the table.
colgroup - Element for grouping columns in a table for styling or semantic purposes.
col - Element representing one or more columns within a colgroup. Self-closing.
map - Element defining an image map with clickable areas on an image.
area - Element defining clickable areas within an image map. Self-closing.
noscript - Element containing content to display when JavaScript is disabled or not supported.
keywords.example: This page requires JavaScript to function properly.

keywords.description: script - Element for embedding or referencing JavaScript code in HTML documents.
style - Element containing CSS style information for the HTML document.
link - Element for linking external resources like stylesheets, icons, or prefetch resources. Self-closing.
meta - Element providing metadata about the HTML document like charset, viewport, or description. Self-closing.
base - Element specifying base URL for all relative URLs in the document. Self-closing.
head - Container element for metadata and resources that aren't displayed in the document body.
body - Container element for all visible content of the HTML document.
html - Root element of an HTML document, containing all other elements.
title - Element defining the document title shown in browser title bar or page tab.
<template> - Holds HTML content not rendered on load. Used for client-side instantiation via JavaScript.
<slot> - Placeholder inside a web component's shadow DOM. Named slots target specific content.
<data> - Links content with a machine-readable value attribute.
<bdi> - Bidirectional Isolate. Isolates text that may be formatted in a different direction.
<bdo> - Bidirectional Override. Overrides text directionality with dir attribute.
<ruby> - Container for ruby annotations, used for East Asian typography pronunciation guides.
<rt> - Defines the pronunciation/annotation text in a ruby annotation.
<rp> - Fallback parentheses for browsers that don't support ruby annotations.
<s> - Strikethrough text, indicating content that is no longer accurate or relevant.
<u> - Unarticulated annotation. Renders underlined text for proper nouns in Chinese or spelling errors.
<menu> - Semantic alternative to <ul> for interactive lists of commands/actions.
<search> - Semantic container for search functionality. Replaces <div role="search">. New in HTML5.
<hgroup> - Groups a heading with related subheadings or taglines.
<portal> - Embeds another page for seamless navigation transitions (experimental).
class - Space-separated list of CSS class names for styling and JavaScript selection.
id - Unique identifier for the element. Used for CSS, JS, and fragment links.
hidden - Boolean attribute. Hides the element from rendering. Can be 'until-found' for searchable hidden content.
keywords.example:
Not visible
Find-in-page can reveal this

keywords.description: tabindex - Controls focus order. 0=natural order, -1=programmatic only, positive=explicit order (avoid).
contenteditable - Makes the element editable by the user. Values: true, false, plaintext-only.
draggable - Makes the element draggable via the Drag and Drop API.
lang - Declares the language of the element's content. Critical for accessibility and SEO.
dir - Text directionality: ltr, rtl, or auto (detects from content).
data-* - Custom data attributes for storing extra information on elements. Accessed via dataset API.
role - ARIA role defining the element's purpose for assistive technology.
aria-label - Defines an accessible name for elements without visible text labels.
aria-labelledby - References another element's ID as the accessible label. Preferred over aria-label when visible text exists.
aria-describedby - References an element providing additional descriptive text.
keywords.example: Must be at least 8 characters

keywords.description: aria-hidden - Hides element from the accessibility tree. Use "true" for decorative elements.
aria-live - Indicates a live region that announces dynamic content changes. Values: off, polite, assertive.
aria-expanded - Indicates whether a collapsible element is expanded (true) or collapsed (false).
aria-controls - Identifies the element(s) controlled by the current element.
aria-current - Indicates the current item in a set. Values: page, step, location, date, time, true.
aria-required - Indicates that user input is required. Prefer the HTML required attribute when possible.
aria-invalid - Indicates the element's value does not conform to expected format.
required - Boolean. Form control must have a value before submission.
placeholder - Short hint displayed when the input is empty. Not a substitute for <label>.
autofocus - Boolean. Automatically focuses the element on page load. Use only once per page.
autocomplete - Hint for browser autofill. Values: on, off, name, email, tel, address-line1, etc.
disabled - Boolean. Disables the form control. Not submitted with the form.
readonly - Boolean. Makes input non-editable but still submitted with form data.
pattern - Regex pattern the input value must match for validation.
min - Minimum value for number, range, date, and time inputs.
max - Maximum value for number, range, date, and time inputs.
step - Granularity of input values. Use 'any' for no stepping constraint.
multiple - Allows multiple values for file inputs and select elements.
accept - Filters file types for file inputs. Use MIME types or extensions.
novalidate - Skips form validation on submission. Used on <form> element.
formaction - Overrides the form's action URL for this submit button.
Single-line text input. Default input type.
Email input with built-in validation. Shows email keyboard on mobile.
Password input. Characters are masked. Use autocomplete="new-password" or "current-password".
Numeric input with spinner. Shows numeric keyboard on mobile.
Telephone input. Shows phone keypad on mobile. No built-in validation.
URL input with protocol validation. Shows URL keyboard on mobile.
Search input. May show a clear button. Styled differently on some platforms.
Date picker. Value format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date and time picker without timezone. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM.
Time picker. Value format: HH:MM or HH:MM:SS.
Color picker. Value is a hex color string like #rrggbb.
Slider input. Imprecise numeric input between min and max.
File upload input. Use accept to filter types, multiple for multi-select.
Checkbox input. Boolean toggle. Can be indeterminate via JavaScript.
Radio button. One selection per group (same name attribute).
Hidden input. Submitted with form but not visible. For CSRF tokens, IDs, etc.
Submit button that sends the form. Prefer <button> element instead.
rel - Relationship between the current document and the linked resource.
href - URL of the linked resource. Used on <a>, <link>, <area>, <base>.
target - Where to open the linked URL: _self, _blank, _parent, _top, or frame name.
rel="noopener" - Prevents the opened page from accessing window.opener. Security best practice with target="_blank".
loading - Lazy-load images and iframes. Values: lazy (defer), eager (immediate).
decoding - Image decode hint: sync, async, or auto. async avoids blocking rendering.
fetchpriority - Resource fetch priority: high, low, auto. Use high for LCP images.
srcset - Set of image sources with width descriptors or pixel density for responsive images.
sizes - Media conditions with image display size, used with srcset width descriptors.
alt - Alternative text for images. Required for accessibility. Empty string for decorative images.
width - Intrinsic width of image/video/canvas. Prevents layout shift when set with height.
height - Intrinsic height of image/video/canvas. Set with width to reserve space.
preload - Media preload hint: none, metadata, auto. Controls how much is fetched before play.
autoplay - Starts media playback automatically. Muted required for autoplay without user interaction.
controls - Shows browser's built-in media player controls.
playsinline - Plays video inline on mobile instead of fullscreen. Required for iOS autoplay.
poster - Image shown before video playback starts.
sandbox - Restricts iframe capabilities. Values: allow-scripts, allow-same-origin, allow-forms, etc.
charset - Character encoding declaration. Must be UTF-8. Must be within first 1024 bytes.
viewport meta - Controls the viewport for responsive design on mobile.
og:title - Open Graph title for social media sharing previews.
og:description - Open Graph description for social media cards.
og:image - Open Graph image URL for social media share cards. Recommended: 1200×630.
theme-color - Sets the browser's address bar / UI color on mobile.
meta description - Page description used by search engines in results snippets.
keywords.example: <meta name="description" content="A brief page description for SEO">

keywords.description: popover - Native popover behavior. Values: auto (light-dismiss) or manual. No JS needed.
popovertarget - References the popover element to toggle by ID.
popovertargetaction - Controls popover behavior: toggle (default), show, or hide.
open - Boolean attribute. Shows the dialog/details element. Use showModal() for modal dialogs.
inert - Makes element and all descendants non-interactive and invisible to assistive technology.

snippets.description: Complete HTML5 document template with proper DOCTYPE and meta tags
snippets.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="${1:en}">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>${2:Document Title}</title>
</head>
<body>
    ${3:<!-- Page content -->}
</body>
</html>

snippets.description: JSON-LD structured data for SEO
snippets.content:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "${1:Article}",
  "headline": "${2:Article Title}",
  "description": "${3:Article description}",
  "image": {
    "@type": "ImageObject",
    "url": "${4:https://example.com/image.jpg}",
    "width": ${5:1200},
    "height": ${6:630}
  },
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "${7:Author Name}",
    "url": "${8:https://example.com/author}"
  },
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "${9:Publisher Name}",
    "logo": {
      "@type": "ImageObject",
      "url": "${10:https://example.com/logo.png}",
      "width": ${11:300},
      "height": ${12:60}
    }
  },
  "datePublished": "${13:2024-01-01T09:00:00+00:00}",
  "dateModified": "${14:2024-01-01T09:00:00+00:00}",
  "mainEntityOfPage": {
    "@type": "WebPage",
    "@id": "${15:https://example.com/article}"
  }
}
</script>

snippets.description: Progressive Web App manifest and service worker registration
snippets.content:
<!-- PWA Manifest -->
<link rel="manifest" href="${1:/manifest.json}">
<meta name="theme-color" content="${2:#000000}">
<meta name="background-color" content="${3:#ffffff}">

<!-- iOS specific meta tags -->
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="${4:default}">
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="${5:App Name}">
<link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="${6:/icons/apple-touch-icon.png}">

<!-- Service Worker Registration -->
<script>
if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
    window.addEventListener('load', () => {
        navigator.serviceWorker.register('${7:/sw.js}')
            .then(registration => {
                console.log('SW registered: ', registration);
            })
            .catch(registrationError => {
                console.log('SW registration failed: ', registrationError);
            });
    });
}
</script>

snippets.description: Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags for social sharing
snippets.content:
<!-- Open Graph -->
<meta property="og:title" content="${1:Title}">
<meta property="og:description" content="${2:Description}">
<meta property="og:image" content="${3:https://example.com/image.jpg}">
<meta property="og:url" content="${4:https://example.com}">
<meta property="og:type" content="${5:website}">

<!-- Twitter Card -->
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
<meta name="twitter:title" content="${1:Title}">
<meta name="twitter:description" content="${2:Description}">
<meta name="twitter:image" content="${3:https://example.com/image.jpg}">

}
</script>
